rationale: Both notes indicate strong conceptual overlap in dyadic WOM transmission on social media (citing paper: sender-receiver overlap and sharing hazard; cited paper: sender-recipient closeness and WOM valence/mechanisms). The citing note explicitly says it extends prior WOM and dyadic sharing research, making this cited study a plausible theory/background anchor rather than a purely incidental mention. With only DOI-level match and no in-text snippet, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only.
